title: sports-registry — outbound webhooks
description: Tenant admins can subscribe to sport-catalog-lifecycle events via the webhook module (shared/webhooks, Wave 4). This blueprint declares the event types this module publishes. Payload shapes mirror the corresponding entries in `events.json` — the webhook module wraps them in the standard envelope.

event_types.description: New Sport row created. Payload includes name, slug, category, governing_body, gender_typical, is_platform_seeded, source.
Sport is_active flipped from true → false. Payload includes downstream_reference_count.
The platform catalog was seeded. Fires at platform first-boot + on explicit reseed. Payload includes { catalog_version, seeded_by, sport_count, discipline_count, position_count, duration_ms }.
The platform catalog was updated (adds / updates / deactivates). Fires on reseed with force. Payload includes summary lists of added / updated / deactivated slugs per level.

A downstream row references a sport_key / discipline_key / position_key that failed to resolve. Payload includes { source_table, source_row_id, source_column, unresolved_value, resolver_context }. Audit-critical.

notes: Every payload is signed with the tenant's webhook secret (HMAC-SHA256) per the shared webhook contract.
Sport / Discipline / Position rows carry no PII — payloads are safe to route to any tenant-approved webhook destination.

The prefix_resolution_failed event MAY carry a source_row_id that hints at a specific enrollment / team; downstream security review confirms this is not PII (the ID is a ULID, not a name / email / phone). Tenants routing this event to third-party ops tools should ensure the destination is authorised to see internal ULIDs.
